allocations.rationale: Sharpe 1.22 | Alpha +18.62% — Top-quintile risk-adjusted return; beta 1.11 (takes full market upside); 3y CAGR 26.9%. Genuine active alpha, not fee drag.
Sharpe 1.00 | Alpha +14.48% — Lower volatility (β=0.89) than mid-cap category avg, yet 5y CAGR 21.8%. Largest AUM (₹243Cr) = stability.
Sharpe 1.36 | Alpha +23.22% — Highest Sharpe in mid-cap universe. 3y CAGR 27.8%. Information ratio 2.58 (genuine stock-picking edge). Beta 0.90 = lower drawdown.
Sharpe 0.90 | Alpha +10.78% — 5y CAGR 22.2%, β=1.05 (appropriate for small cap), TE 10.5% (active management, not closet indexing). Solid alpha in small-cap segment.
Sharpe 2.65 | ER 0.20% — Exceptional Sharpe ratio (market-leading). International diversification (USD exposure, ₹ hedge). 1y return 34.2%, 3y 23.8%. Beta 0.07 (uncorrelated to Nifty).
Core stability. 5y CAGR 15.4%, Sharpe 0.43 (lower risk volatility σ=13.7%). Acts as portfolio ballast against equity drawdowns. Largest AUM (₹248Cr).
Sharpe 0.78 | Alpha +7.24% — 5y CAGR 21.3%, balanced exposure across cap sizes. Lower tracking error (6.4%) suggests disciplined implementation.
Tax efficiency: ₹1.5L Section 80C deduction (saves ₹45k tax at 30% slab annually). Sharpe 0.46, 3y CAGR 21.0%. Post-3y lock-in, gains are equity-taxed at 12.5% (vs 30% for debt).

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open Direct Mutual Fund Accounts on Kuvera, Groww, or MFU (direct plans, no distributor fees). Link PAN and bank account. Step 2: Deploy initial ₹40L lumpsum within 1 week across 8 funds per allocation table (₹8L to Motilal L&M, ₹6L to HDFC Mid Cap, ₹6L to WhiteOak Mid Cap, ₹6L to Invesco Smallcap, ₹4L to S&P 500 Index, ₹4L to ICICI Large Cap, ₹4L to Nippon Multi Cap, ₹2L to WhiteOak ELSS). Step 3: Set up monthly SIPs (₹1.5L total) automating into each fund per allocation percentages (₹30k to Motilal L&M, ₹22.5k each to HDFC Mid Cap / WhiteOak Mid Cap / Invesco Smallcap, ₹15k each to S&P 500 / ICICI Large Cap / Nippon Multi Cap, ₹7.5k to WhiteOak ELSS). Step 4: Tax-optimize ELSS by investing ₹1.5L annually in January each year to maximize Section 80C deduction (saves ₹45k tax at 30% slab annually). Lock-in 3 years; post-unlock gains are equity-taxed at 12.5% on amounts >₹1.25L.
review_checkpoints: Quarterly (every 3 months) review: Monitor each fund's 3m, 6m, 1y returns vs. category median; exit if underperforms peer median for 2+ consecutive quarters or if manager changes / style drift detected
Sharpe Ratio Degradation: Exit if Sharpe drops >0.2 points while category Sharpe remains stable; signals loss of risk-adjusted edge
Tracking Error Explosion: Flag if TE rises >30% above historical level (signals rogue position-taking); exit if unexplained
Portfolio Beta Drift: Exit and replace if beta <0.9 for 12+ months (suggests manager playing too conservatively, missing upside)
SIP Continuity: Reconcile bank statements monthly; fix any failed ₹1.5L monthly debits same day to maintain dollar-cost averaging discipline
Portfolio Rebalancing Drift: Rebalance quarterly if any position drifts >2 percentage points from target allocation; sell winners, buy losers to lock gains
Post-3-Year ELSS Unlock: After 3-year lock-in, exit WhiteOak ELSS and reallocate proceeds to equity fund with highest current Sharpe ratio to maintain active alpha generation
plan.rationale: This is a concentrated, conviction-driven portfolio of 8 funds selected on two equally-weighted criteria: (1) Sharpe ratio / risk-adjusted return and (2) alpha generation relative to category peer medians. Portfolio-weighted post-tax CAGR is 13.3% (after 30% tax slab, 12.5% LTCG on equity gains). Expected terminal value at 18-year horizon: ₹12.85Cr (₹85L above ₹12Cr goal). Allocation balances alpha-generating active managers (Motilal Oswal L&M: +18.62% alpha, HDFC Mid Cap: +14.48% alpha, WhiteOak Mid Cap: +23.22% alpha) with portfolio stability anchors (ICICI Large Cap: Sharpe 0.43, largest AUM stability) and international uncorrelated hedge (S&P 500 Index: Sharpe 2.65, β=0.07 to Nifty). 100% allocation to equity-taxed funds (≥65% equity exposure) to exploit 12.5% LTCG tax advantage vs. 30% slab-taxed debt instruments. Portfolio beta ≈0.92 provides full market participation (beta 1.11 in Motilal L&M) while reducing volatility vs. pure small-cap exposure. SIP-based rupee-cost averaging over ₹18L/year initial commitment ($1.5L/month) compounds wealth via market cycles. ELSS allocation provides ₹1.5L annual Section 80C deduction (saves ₹45k tax/year at 30% slab). Quantitative discipline: Quarterly monitoring of Sharpe, alpha, tracking error; exit thresholds include Sharpe degradation >0.2pp or 2 consecutive quarters of category underperformance. Rebalancing ±2pp drift maintains target risk profile. Suitable for high-net-worth, aggressive-appetite, long-horizon investor (18 years) seeking tax-efficient wealth accumulation.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

aps.reasoning: passive_instrument_fraction: Only 1 of 8 funds is a passive index instrument (Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index, 10% allocation). The remaining 90% of equity AUM is in actively managed funds (Large & Midcap, Midcap, Smallcap, Multicap, ELSS, Large Cap). Score = 0.10. turnover_score: The plan mandates quarterly reviews with explicit exit triggers (2 consecutive quarters of underperformance, Sharpe degradation >0.2pp, TE explosion, beta drift), plus quarterly rebalancing at ±2pp drift. This is moderately active — not monthly trading but more intervention than annual passive rebalancing. Score = 0.30. cost_emphasis_score: The plan does mention direct plans and no distributor fees, and notes low ER on the S&P 500 fund (0.20%), but the primary selection criteria are explicitly Sharpe ratio and alpha generation, not cost minimisation. Expense ratios are listed but not the dominant filter. Score = 0.25. research_vs_cost_score: Significant fund-level quantitative research is embedded — Sharpe ratios, alpha vs. category peers, information ratios, beta, tracking error, and AUM stability are all evaluated per fund. No individual stock analysis, but clear active due diligence on manager quality and risk-adjusted metrics. Score = 0.20. time_horizon_alignment_score: An 18-year terminal goal is stated, and SIP compounding over market cycles is emphasised. However, quarterly monitoring with aggressive exit triggers and post-ELSS reallocation based on 'current Sharpe' introduces short-term tactical behaviour. Partially long-term but operationally active. Score = 0.50. portfolio_activeness_score: The equity portfolio is dominated by genuinely active funds with very high alpha (23.22%, 18.62%, 14.48%, 10.78%, 7.24% vs Nifty 50 proxy), high tracking errors where disclosed (10.5% for Invesco, 6.4% for Nippon), an information ratio of 2.58 for WhiteOak Mid Cap, and betas slightly above or below 1.0. The rationale explicitly celebrates 'genuine stock-picking edge' and 'not closet indexing.' This is a deeply active portfolio. Score = 0.10.

pqs.reasoning: GOAL ALIGNMENT (0.80): The plan directly targets ₹12.85Cr terminal value vs the ₹12Cr goal, provides Sharpe-ratio-driven fund selection matching the investor's stated optimisation preference, includes international diversification, and quantifies per-fund alpha. However, sip_step_up is null — a significant omission for an 18-year compounder with high income growth potential, and the 'fund research institute' goal receives no treatment. Some data fields (NAV=0, missing 5y returns, missing volatility for most funds) weaken the investor's expectation of a fully quantitative rationale. DIVERSIFICATION (0.70): Covers five distinct equity categories plus international (S&P 500), and uses six separate fund houses. However, the portfolio is 100% equity with zero fixed-income or alternative allocation; even aggressive portfolios at 18-year horizons often carry a small rebalancing buffer. International exposure is US-only (10%), ignoring other geographies the investor explicitly wanted. Concentration in mid/small caps (~45% combined) is valid for aggressive appetite but amplifies drawdown risk that is never quantified. RISK-RETURN APPROPRIATENESS (0.65): Risk level is broadly appropriate — 100% equity for aggressive, 18-year horizon is defensible. Post-tax CAGR of 13.3% and ₹12.85Cr terminal value are plausible. But the 'risks' array is entirely empty — a critical failure for an investor who explicitly demands downside scenario modelling. No max-drawdown or worst-case Monte Carlo is presented. ICICI Prudential Large Cap (Sharpe 0.43) undercuts the stated Sharpe-maximisation objective. INTERNAL CONSISTENCY (0.72): All allocation percentages sum to 100%, SIP amounts total ₹1.5L, and lumpsum totals ₹40L — arithmetic is clean. Review checkpoints describe rebalancing rules, but the rebalancing_guidelines field is blank. 'risks' is empty despite the rationale referencing risk management. The claim that ICICI Large Cap is 'portfolio ballast' is in tension with a Sharpe-optimisation mandate — its 0.43 Sharpe is never defended quantitatively. TAX EFFICIENCY (0.70): Correctly includes ELSS for ₹1.5L 80C deduction saving ₹45k/year at 30% slab — appropriate for old-regime investor. Explicitly avoids debt/slab-taxed instruments and justifies 12.5% LTCG rates. However, the plan incorrectly claims '100% allocation to equity-taxed funds' — the Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index fund invests in foreign equities and does NOT qualify as an equity fund under Indian MF taxation rules (requires ≥65% domestic equity). Post-Budget 2024, it is taxed as a non-equity fund (LTCG at 12.5% only after 24 months, STCG at slab rate), not at the 1-year equity LTCG threshold. This misstates the tax treatment for 10% of the portfolio for a 30% bracket investor — a material error.
